Job Title: UX Designer
Department: Product Design Reports To: Head of Design / Senior Product Manager

Company Overview
[Insert Company Name] is a forward-thinking technology company dedicated to solving [specific problem/industry challenge]. We believe that great technology should be invisible, intuitive, and accessible. Our team is a blend of creative thinkers and analytical problem-solvers who are passionate about building products that users love. We are currently scaling our operations and are looking for a dedicated UX Designer to help us bridge the gap between complex functionality and seamless user experience.

The Role
We are seeking a user-obsessed UX Designer who thrives at the intersection of psychology, design, and technology. In this role, you will not just be designing screens; you will be solving behavioral problems. You will be responsible for the full lifecycle of the user experience—from initial research and discovery to wireframing, prototyping, and final usability testing.

You will act as the "Voice of the User" within the product team, advocating for human-centered design principles while balancing business goals and technical feasibility. You will work closely with Product Managers to define the "what" and Engineers to understand the "how," while you own the "why" and "where."

Key Responsibilities
Discovery & Research: Lead and conduct user research initiatives, including user interviews, surveys, and competitive audits. You will synthesize qualitative and quantitative data to create user personas, empathy maps, and journey maps that guide design decisions.

Information Architecture & Interaction: Translate abstract concepts into clear, logical user flows and information architectures. You will ensure that navigation is intuitive and that users can accomplish their goals with minimal friction.

Prototyping & Iteration: Create low-fidelity wireframes for quick feedback and high-fidelity interactive prototypes for detailed testing. You will iterate relentlessly based on stakeholder feedback and usability test results.

Usability Testing: Plan and moderate usability testing sessions. You will analyze the results to identify pain points and areas for improvement, implementing changes that measurably enhance the user experience.

Collaboration: Partner with UI Designers to ensure the visual interface supports the user experience. Collaborate with developers during the hand-off process to ensure accurate implementation of interactions and states.

Qualifications & Skills
Experience: 3+ years of professional experience in UX Design, Product Design, or Interaction Design.

Portfolio: A comprehensive portfolio is required. We want to see case studies that showcase your process, not just final UI shots. Show us how you identified a problem, how you researched it, your sketches, your failures, and your final solution.

Technical Proficiency: Expert-level proficiency in Figma (including auto-layout, components, and prototyping). Experience with tools like Miro, FigJam, or Maze is a plus.

Analytical Thinking: Ability to use data (Google Analytics, Mixpanel, etc.) to inform design decisions.

Communication: Strong storytelling skills. You must be able to articulate the rationale behind your design decisions to non-design stakeholders clearly and persuasively.
